summary.headline: Best opportunity today: MTD onboarding and cashflow automation, with AI admin services still attractive but slightly softer on proof.
summary.best_opportunity: Making Tax Digital onboarding, records cleanup and lightweight filing automation for sole traders, landlords and small accountancy firms
summary.why_now: HMRC's 5 February 2026 update said 864,000 sole traders and landlords were entering Making Tax Digital for Income Tax from 6 April 2026, with later waves at £30,000 income from 6 April 2027 and £20,000 from 6 April 2028. That creates live compliance demand, fast service revenue and an obvious recurring support layer.

top_opportunities.name: Making Tax Digital onboarding and automation for sole traders, landlords and accountants
top_opportunities.description: Sell a fixed-scope package that cleans records, connects bookkeeping software, sets reminder workflows and provides ongoing quarterly support for MTD for Income Tax.
top_opportunities.why_now: Live HMRC guidance and a 5 February 2026 GOV.UK news update confirm the 6 April 2026 rollout for qualifying income above £50,000, with £30,000 following in 2027 and £20,000 in 2028. The need is real, deadline-led and service-first rather than speculative.
top_opportunities.buyer: Sole traders, portfolio landlords, small bookkeepers and small accountancy practices
top_opportunities.recurring_revenue: High - setup fee plus monthly or quarterly support
top_opportunities.founder_fit: Excellent for a solo engineer because the first offer is workflow setup, dashboards and integrations rather than building a full accounting platform.
risks: Accounting software brands already own core bookkeeping mindshare, so the wedge must be migration and admin reduction
The offer should stay on setup and process automation, not regulated tax advice
top_opportunities.verdict: Best overall because the trigger is mandatory, fragmented and already active in-market.

top_opportunities.name: Invoice chasing and e-invoicing automation for UK SMEs
top_opportunities.description: Build a productized service or micro-SaaS that generates cleaner invoices, automates reminders, tracks promises to pay and gives owners a simple overdue cash dashboard.
top_opportunities.why_now: The Small Business Commissioner homepage currently says late payments cost the UK economy £11 billion per year. Its e-invoicing guidance says moving to e-invoicing can reduce invoicing costs by 60 to 80 percent, with cited examples of faster processing and fewer supplier queries. That is a direct cashflow pain with clear ROI.
top_opportunities.buyer: Micro businesses, agencies, consultancies, trades firms and small B2B service companies
top_opportunities.recurring_revenue: High - monthly usage fee or managed receivables service
top_opportunities.founder_fit: Very strong for a solo engineer because this is mainly integrations, reminders, templates and reporting rather than deep domain software.
risks: Some buyers will assume their accounting package already solves this unless the pitch is framed around debtor days and cash collection
Access to accounting data can be messy across Xero, QuickBooks and spreadsheet-led workflows
top_opportunities.verdict: One of the best fast-cash opportunities because the value is immediate and easy for owners to understand.

top_opportunities.name: AI lead-response and admin automation for trades and local service firms
top_opportunities.description: Productize missed-call text-back, instant web-form replies, quote follow-up, scheduling handoff and simple reporting for firms that lose work through slow response times.
top_opportunities.why_now: Evidence is solid on the pain but softer on niche-by-niche buying intent. ONS' 19 March 2026 business insights bulletin says cost of labour was the most reported concern among trading businesses at 35 percent, while the government's AI Opportunities Action Plan explicitly pushes AI adoption across the economy to boost growth and productivity. That supports a practical sell: recover leads and reduce office admin.
top_opportunities.buyer: Plumbers, electricians, HVAC firms, clinics, salons, cleaning firms and other local service operators
top_opportunities.founder_fit: Strong for a solo engineer because the stack is mostly messaging, CRM glue, forms and dashboards.
risks: There is visible competition, so a narrow niche and hard ROI framing are essential
Some local firms have weak lead tracking, making proof of value harder until you instrument the funnel
top_opportunities.verdict: Strong if sold as recovered jobs and saved admin hours, not generic AI consulting.

top_opportunities.name: SME tender-monitoring and bid-drafting assistant for public sector work
top_opportunities.description: Create a niche service or tool that monitors relevant tenders, scores fit, drafts first-pass answers and maintains a reusable evidence library for small suppliers.
top_opportunities.why_now: The Cabinet Office and HM Treasury SME Action Plan for 2025 to 2028 says departments now have SME targets and that the Procurement Act 2023 creates a more simple and transparent regime, making it easier for SMEs to do business with government. That is a credible signal for a focused support tool, though cash may be slower than compliance or invoicing services.
top_opportunities.buyer: Small consultancies, digital agencies, specialist service firms and compliance-heavy B2B suppliers
top_opportunities.recurring_revenue: Medium to high - retainer, seat fee or managed bid support
top_opportunities.founder_fit: Good for an engineer who can combine tender scraping, search, document generation and workflow tooling into a narrow offer.
risks: Sales cycles can be slower because tendering is episodic
Bid-writing agencies already exist, so the offer needs automation leverage or a strict niche focus
top_opportunities.verdict: Promising niche B2B play, best if you own one supplier category rather than the whole procurement market.

top_opportunities.name: Cyber Essentials readiness and security workflow automation for small organisations
top_opportunities.description: Offer a fixed-scope package covering backups, account hygiene, device checks, staff prompts and evidence collection for small organisations that need a cleaner security baseline.
top_opportunities.why_now: The NCSC small organisations guide was reviewed on 9 April 2026 and is explicitly written for small and medium sized organisations, with adjacent guidance for self-employed and sole traders. That keeps cyber hygiene demand credible, although evidence for fastest buying urgency is weaker than for tax or cashflow pain.
top_opportunities.buyer: Small professional firms, charities, clinics, local agencies and B2B service SMEs
top_opportunities.recurring_revenue: Medium to high - monitoring, updates and periodic reviews
top_opportunities.founder_fit: Good if you like structured technical delivery and can keep the service checklist-driven.
risks: Trust matters in security work, so some buyers will prefer established specialists
If the offer becomes too bespoke, part-time solo delivery gets much harder
top_opportunities.verdict: Viable as a productized operations package, but less immediate than the top two opportunities.

best_pick.name: Making Tax Digital onboarding and automation for sole traders, landlords and accountants
best_pick.why_it_wins: It has the clearest live trigger, the easiest service-first launch under a £2,000 budget and the cleanest path from one-off setup work into recurring quarterly support. Compared with broader AI services, the buyer pain is less abstract and easier to sell this week.
validation_plan: Pick one wedge first: landlords with simple property income records or small accountancy firms that need an MTD migration helper for clients.
Build a tiny demo showing document capture, quarterly reminders and a client-status dashboard using off-the-shelf bookkeeping software plus lightweight automation.
Publish a fixed-price MTD readiness package with clear scope, turnaround and a monthly support option.
Contact 30 to 50 accountants, bookkeepers and landlord-facing businesses before writing custom software beyond onboarding and reporting.
best_pick.first_offer: MTD readiness setup for sole traders and landlords: records cleanup, software connection, reminder workflows and first-quarter support. Charge roughly £299 to £799 setup plus £39 to £149 per month depending on complexity.
best_pick.mvp: A thin internal tool plus off-the-shelf bookkeeping software, document capture, reminder automation and a simple dashboard for onboarding status and quarterly check-ins.
customer_types: Sole traders above the MTD threshold
best_pick.do_today: Create a one-page MTD readiness offer, build a dashboard mock-up and start direct outreach to local accountants and landlord-focused businesses today.
